items.text: 1. Forks resolved: decisions.md/plan.md record a decision + one-line rationale on each of (a) representation = B (per-iteration `type`); (b) type vocabulary + per-template-ness; (c) ordering - gate vs convention; (d) per-type DoD-skeletons; (e) plan-tracking = living plan.md with derived Next + "step N of M", items linked to iterations, changes made by edit + logged why; (f) multi-workstream = queue + explicit/default target, retiring `.lume/current`, with a logged note that this supersedes 001's single-current. (Evidence: decisions section covering a-f.)

2. Schemas defined (parseable later): plan.md specifies a plan item's shape (id, type, DoD sketch, status, linked iteration id) regular enough to parse deterministically, and states how done/next/position compute from it. (Evidence: schema + worked example in plan.md.)

3. Execution planned as an ordered list in that schema: each planned iteration has id, `type`, a binary checkable DoD sketch, dependency, and a committed/optional tag. The list must include (i) the queue/multi-workstream rework (retire current; explicit/default target; `lume status` = queue), noting it reworks 001; (ii) un-hardcode `type` in `lume open`; (iii) derive Next + plan-position from the living plan. Per-type DoD-skeletons and the two-template contract proof present as committed or optional. (Evidence: ordered plan; every DoD sketch binary.)

4. First execution iteration openable now: whichever planning sequences first is specified tightly enough to `lume open` next with only minor DoD refinement. (Evidence: that entry is concrete and self-contained.)
5. Constitutional gate applied: every planned iteration tagged committed/optional with reason; ceremony justified by the time it buys back. (Evidence: tags + justification on every item.)
6. Scope held; logged; no code: no engine change; plan.md + a new decisions.md are the only new artifacts (plus the auto-refreshed snapshot). (Evidence: git scope.)

handback:
What changed (uncommitted):
- New `plan.md`: the living forward plan - a parseable item schema + derived done/next/position rules, and the ordered execution plan P1 (queue/multi-workstream rework, reworks 001) -> P2 (un-hardcode `type`) -> P3 (derive Next from the plan) -> P4 (per-type DoD-skeletons) -> P5 (templates + live contract proof, optional/cuttable) -> P6 (close-out), each tagged committed/optional with a sequencing rationale.
- New `decisions.md`: decisions (a)-(f) with rationale, and the logged 001-supersede.
- No engine code touched.
Next iteration to open: P1 (becomes iteration 004) - the queue + multi-workstream rework.
self_review:
Adversarial pass against the artifacts (decisions.md, plan.md):
1. PASS - decisions.md records a decision + rationale for each of (a)-(f), plus an explicit logged note that (f) supersedes 001's single-current (what is kept vs replaced). No fork left open.
2. PASS - plan.md "Schema" defines the parseable line format `- P<n> | <type> | iter:<NNN|-> | <committed|optional> | <sketch>` and the derived done/next/position rules (done = linked iteration accepted; next = first not-done; position = step N of M).
3. PASS - plan.md "Items" P1-P6 are ordered in the schema, each with id/type/iter-link/tag/DoD sketch. Includes the three required: (i) P1 queue + multi-workstream rework, stating it reworks 001; (ii) P2 un-hardcode `type`; (iii) P3 derive Next from the living plan. Per-type DoD-skeletons = P4; two-template contract proof = P5 (optional). DoD sketches for P1-P4 are binary (end in a checkable condition + "tests green"); P5/P6 sketches are intentionally higher-level (P5 is optional; P6 is a retro) and will be made fully binary at open - flagged honestly.
4. PASS - P1 is concrete and self-contained (retire current; `-w <slug>` target + sole-active default + ambiguity error; `lume status` = queue; `new`/`close` drop the cursor; single-active flows preserved; tests). Openable as iteration 004 with only minor DoD refinement.
5. PASS - every item carries a committed/optional tag; "Sequencing rationale" justifies the order against the constitution and names P5 as the designated cut under pressure; a "Deferred" section keeps option A and out-of-scope items out unless a puller appears.
6. PASS - no engine code or behaviour change; only plan.md + the new decisions.md were added (plus this iteration file + the auto-refreshed snapshot). `git status` shows no new engine files beyond accepted-001's. Diff swept: both artifacts are prose - no code.

Note (honest): the snapshot's `## Next` is still hand-authored until P3 ships; this iteration points it at the plan, and P3 makes it derived.
